title: arctostaphylos columbiana f. tracyi
intro: Arctostaphylos columbiana f. tracyi, also known as the greenleaf manzanita, is a shrub species within the bearberry family (Ericaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of greenleaf manzanita include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Greenleaf manzanita can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which are typically used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
